---
title: "Lecture 04: Robot Modeling III - Velocity Kinematics and Inverse Kinematics"
author: "Yonghyeon Lee"
date: today
---

## Learning goals

- Relate joint velocities to task-space velocities.
- Derive and interpret manipulator Jacobians.
- Solve inverse kinematics iteratively.

## Differential kinematics

\[
\dot{x} = J(q)\dot{q}
\]

- \(J(q)\): local mapping from joint to task space
- Singularities reduce directional controllability

## Inverse kinematics idea

\[
\dot{q} \approx J(q)^\dagger \dot{x}_{des}
\]

- Pseudoinverse enables numerical IK updates.
- Damped variants improve behavior near singularity.

## Summary

- Jacobians connect geometry and motion.
- IK turns task goals into executable joint commands.
